playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Nicki Nicole is a rapper and singer from Rosario, Argentina. She blends trap, R&B, and pop music, creating honest songs about love, life, and her rapid rise to fame.
playlist.title: Nicki Nicole: Tiny Desk (Home) Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: John Legend is an American singer, songwriter, and pianist known for blending soul and R&B. He is an EGOT winner and is married to model Chrissy Teigen.
playlist.title: John Legend: NPR Music Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Taylor Swift is an American singer born in 1989. She started her career in country music and later became a pop star, known for her songs about love and life. She has won numerous Grammy Awards and consistently sells out major tours.
playlist.title: Taylor Swift: NPR Music Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Dua Lipa is a British-Albanian singer born in 1995. She gained fame with hits like "New Rules" and albums such as *Future Nostalgia*. Renowned for her husky voice and dance-pop style, she has won multiple Grammy and Brit Awards. In addition to her music career, Dua Lipa also acts and models.
playlist.title: Dua Lipa: Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Miley Cyrus, born in 1992 in Tennessee, USA, gained fame as Hannah Montana on Disney Channel. She later established herself as a pop star with hits such as "Wrecking Ball" and "Flowers." Known for her bold style changes and Grammy wins, she showcases her versatility by both acting and singing a wide range of music.
playlist.title: Miley Cyrus: Tiny Desk (Home) Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Alicia Keys is an American singer, pianist, and actress born in 1981. She is known for blending R&B and soul music. Her hits, such as "Fallin'" and "Empire State of Mind," propelled her to stardom. Keys has won numerous Grammy Awards throughout her career. In addition to her music, she has also acted and created a Broadway musical.
playlist.title: Alicia Keys: NPR Music Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Justin Timberlake, born in 1981, gained fame as a member of the boy band NSYNC. He later became a successful solo pop artist, known for hits like "Cry Me a River." In addition to his music career, Timberlake has acted in films such as Trolls and has won numerous Grammy Awards.
playlist.title: Justin Timberlake: Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Justin Bieber is a well-known Canadian pop singer. Born in 1994, he achieved fame through YouTube covers as a teenager. Hits like "Baby" made him a star. He has won multiple Grammy Awards, and many of his albums have topped the charts.
playlist.title: Justin Bieber: Tiny Desk (Home) Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Lewis Capaldi is a Scottish singer and songwriter known for his emotional pop songs about love and pain. His powerful voice and sincere lyrics resonate with many people across the world.
playlist.title: Lewis Capaldi: Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Becky G, born Rebbeca Marie Gomez in 1997, is an American singer, rapper, and actress. She gained fame through her YouTube covers and hit songs like 'Shower' and 'Mayores'. As a multi-platinum artist, she blends pop music with Latin influences.
playlist.title: Becky G: Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Roberto Fonseca is a Cuban jazz pianist born in 1975 in Havana. As the son of a drummer and singer, he blends Afro-Cuban roots with jazz, hip-hop, and electronica. He is a Grammy-nominated artist known for his tours with the Buena Vista Social Club and has released nine solo albums.
playlist.title: Roberto Fonseca: Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: C. Tangana, whose real name is Antón Álvarez Alfaro, is a renowned Spanish rapper from Madrid. Born in 1990, he initially performed under the name Crema and was a member of the group Agorazein before pursuing a solo career. He is well-known for his albums, especially "El Madrileño," which blends hip-hop, flamenco, and Latin music. Throughout his career, he has received nine Latin Grammy Awards.
playlist.title: C. Tangana: Tiny Desk (Home) Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Juanes is a Colombian rock star born in 1972 in Medellín, known for blending rock with Latin sounds. He has won 26 Latin Grammy Awards. He collaborated with Mon Laferte on hits such as “Amárrame.” Mon Laferte, a Chilean-Mexican singer born in 1983, combines indie, soul, and folk influences. She is recognized for her powerful voice and activism. Together, they have shared a musical bond that spans over a decade.
playlist.title: Juanes & Mon Laferte: NPR Music Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Natalia Lafourcade is a renowned Mexican singer and songwriter. She creates music in various genres, including pop, rock, jazz, and folk. With her soft voice, she composes poetic songs. Having won numerous awards, she is well-known throughout Latin America.
playlist.title: Natalia Lafourcade: NPR Music Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Marta Pereira da Costa, born in Lisbon in 1982, is the first woman to play the Portuguese guitar professionally in fado. After training in piano and guitar, she left her career in civil engineering in 2012 to pursue music. Marta blends fado with jazz and world sounds and has released several acclaimed albums.
playlist.title: Marta Pereira da Costa: Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Dua Lipa is a British-Albanian singer born in 1995. She gained fame with hits like "New Rules" and albums such as *Future Nostalgia*. Renowned for her husky voice and dance-pop style, she has won multiple Grammy and Brit Awards. In addition to her music career, Dua Lipa also acts and models.
playlist.title: Dua Lipa: Tiny Desk (Home) Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Yasmin Williams is a fingerstyle guitarist and composer from Virginia. She plays the guitar on her lap and incorporates a kalimba into her music. Williams has released albums such as "Urban Driftwood" and "Acadia," and she is known for her unique and intuitive music style.
playlist.title: Yasmin Williams: Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Suzanne Vega is an American singer-songwriter born in 1959. She is well-known for her folk songs such as 'Luka' and 'Tom's Diner'. Her music career has spanned over 40 years, during which she has released many successful albums.
playlist.title: Suzanne Vega: NPR Music Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Sabrina Carpenter is an American singer and actress born in 1999. She began her career with roles on Disney and gained popularity through her YouTube covers. Some of her hit songs include "Espresso" and "Please Please Please." Her albums, "Short n' Sweet" and "Man's Best Friend," topped the charts. She has won two Grammy Awards.
playlist.title: Sabrina Carpenter: Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Billie Eilish is a singer and songwriter from Los Angeles, California. She has a soft yet deep voice and collaborates with her brother, Finneas, to write dark pop songs that explore honest emotions.
playlist.title: Billie Eilish: Tiny Desk (Home) Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Nile Rodgers co-founded Chic in 1972 with Bernard Edwards. They created disco-funk hits like 'Le Freak' and 'Good Times'. As a top producer, he worked with David Bowie, Madonna, and Daft Punk, shaping pop music.
playlist.title: Nile Rodgers & CHIC: Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Jenny and the Mexicats is a multicultural band formed in 2008 in Madrid. British singer-trumpeter Jenny Ball leads with Mexican brothers on guitar and bass, plus a Spanish percussionist. They mix flamenco, jazz, folk, cumbia, and rockabilly in English and Spanish songs. Independent label owners, they earned gold in Mexico.
playlist.title: Jenny and the Mexicats: NPR Music Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Camila Cabello is a Cuban-American singer born in 1997. She rose to fame with Fifth Harmony, then went solo. Hits like 'Havana' and 'Señorita' made her a pop star. She mixes pop, R&B, and Latin music.
playlist.title: Camila Cabello: Tiny Desk (Home) Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Karol G, born Carolina Giraldo Navarro in Medellín, Colombia, in 1991, is a top reggaeton singer. She mixes reggaeton, Latin trap, and pop. Hits like 'Tusa' made her a Grammy winner. She broke barriers for women in music.
playlist.title: Karol G: Tiny Desk (Home) Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Harry Styles, born 1994, is an English singer, songwriter, and actor. He rose to fame with One Direction on The X Factor. Now a solo star with hits like Watermelon Sugar. Known for bold fashion and charm.
playlist.title: Harry Styles: NPR Music Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.description: Billie Eilish is a singer and songwriter from Los Angeles, California. She has a soft yet deep voice and collaborates with her brother, Finneas, to write dark pop songs that explore honest emotions.
playlist.title: Billie Eilish: Tiny Desk Concert

playlist.author: NPR Music
playlist.creator: videoPlayer
playlist.description:
Ludovico Einaudi is an Italian composer and pianist who writes simple, emotive music for piano and other instruments. He often uses the piano together with strings, light electronics, or percussion. His pieces can sound like a mix of classical, folk, and modern ambient styles.

SET LIST

"Experience"
"Fly"
"Flora"
"Low Mist"

MUSICIANS

Ludovico Einaudi: piano
Federico Mecozzi: violin
Redi Hasa: cello
Francesco Arcuri: percussion
playlist.title: Ludovico Einaudi: Tiny Desk Concert
